project.name: MissionChief Map Command Toolkit
project.tagline: Operational map command, monitoring and presentation suite for MissionChief.
project.description: A configurable userscript that turns the MissionChief map into an operational command centre with mission intelligence, fleet monitoring, map utilities, responsive layouts, themed interfaces and verified release automation.
project.author: Conroy1988

featureCategories.name: Mission command
featureCategories.description: Prioritise incidents, understand mission state and act without losing map context.

features.name: Mission Age Watch
features.summary: Tracks personal missions by age, severity and clearing state with fast mission controls.
details: Time-ordered mission monitoring with configurable severity thresholds
No Vehicles, Vehicles En-route, Needs Assistance and Clearing states
Responsive positioning for Desktop, Tablet and iOS Mobile Mode

features.name: Critical View
features.summary: Surfaces missions that require attention and keeps mission actions focused.
details: On-demand interface construction to reduce startup cost

features.name: Mission Inspector
features.summary: Opens detailed operational context only when it is needed.

features.name: Major Incident Feed
features.summary: A live top-bar feed for major incidents with quick map navigation.

featureCategories.name: Fleet and transport
featureCategories.description: See vehicle state, transport demand and operational gaps at a glance.

features.name: Vehicle Code Status
features.summary: Summarises vehicle status codes and counts in a compact top-right panel.
details: Persistent toggle and V keyboard shortcut

features.name: Transport Watcher
features.summary: Highlights missions that still require transport and provides live counts.
details: Count summary integrated with operational overlays

features.name: Unit Count and Focus controls
features.summary: Reduces map noise while retaining the operational layers that matter.

features.name: Auto-load all vehicles
features.summary: Loads MissionChief's hidden vehicle batches automatically when an active mission window is opened.
details: Scopes follow-up scans to the active mission window
Includes bounded retries and duplicate-request protection

featureCategories.name: Map operations
featureCategories.description: Make the map faster to read, easier to navigate and more useful during busy sessions.

features.name: Coverage Heat Map
features.summary: Visualises operational coverage directly on the MissionChief map.
details: Works with the Toolkit map controls

features.name: Smart Bookmark Labels
features.summary: Creates compact, readable place labels without losing the full location name.
details: UK-aware place and operational-word abbreviation dictionaries
Desktop tooltips and touch long-press previews

features.name: Coverage rings and map overlays
features.summary: Adds operational overlays and presentation options around the core map.
details: Alliance Buildings map blocker for performance-sensitive pages

featureCategories.name: Finance and presentation
featureCategories.description: Make alliance income, mission completion and payout events easy to understand.

features.name: Alliance Credits
features.summary: Shows alliance mission values with eligibility-aware colouring and value filters.
details: Green when the user has a unit present, yellow when not

features.name: Financial intelligence
features.summary: Summarises income, spending and net position with Discord-ready reporting.

features.name: Payout presentations
features.summary: Displays themed mission-completion banners with optional sound and emergency flash.

featureCategories.name: Responsive operation
featureCategories.description: Use the same Toolkit state across desktop, tablet and iOS layouts.

features.name: Desktop, Tablet and iOS Mobile Mode
features.summary: Purpose-built layouts for full desktop maps, landscape tablets and Safari on iOS.
details: Tablet layout without an unnecessary menu scrollbar

features.name: Settings import and export
features.summary: Moves Toolkit configuration between devices and operating modes.

themes.name: Map Command
themes.description: The default operational command-centre skin: restrained, high-contrast and map focused.

themes.name: Cyberpunk
themes.description: Neon cyan and warning-yellow telemetry with a dense futuristic control aesthetic.

themes.name: Fallout 4
themes.description: Green phosphor terminal styling with worn industrial panels and strong status readouts.

themes.name: Umbrella
themes.description: Clinical black, white and red containment-system presentation.

themes.name: Factorio
themes.description: Heavy industrial controls, amber machinery highlights and mechanical status panels.

themes.name: 007 Intelligence
themes.description: A classified intelligence interface with gun-barrel geometry, dossier styling and champagne-gold controls.

themes.name: Hyrule Command
themes.description: A parchment, royal-gold and ancient-energy command interface with transparent themed artwork.

shortcuts.action: Open or close Vehicle Code Status

modes.name: Desktop
modes.summary: Full map workspace with the complete dock and operational panels.
modes.bestFor: Large displays, mouse and keyboard operation, multi-panel monitoring.

modes.name: Tablet
modes.summary: Landscape-optimised controls that fit the available map area.
modes.bestFor: Touch operation, compact map space and readable persistent controls.

modes.name: iOS Mobile
modes.summary: Safari and Tampermonkey layout with mobile-safe controls and bookmark previews.
modes.bestFor: MissionChief operation from iPhone with minimal map obstruction.

sections.heading: Install through Greasy Fork
sections.body: Install Tampermonkey or a compatible userscript manager, open the Toolkit installation link, review the userscript permissions and confirm installation. Greasy Fork remains the supported update channel.

sections.heading: First launch
sections.body: Open MissionChief and use the map command button to open the Toolkit. The core map control and shortcut bar load first; larger panels and operational monitors are constructed only when required.

sections.heading: Persistent controls
sections.body: Feature state, theme selection, operating mode and toolbar state are retained. Use settings export before substantial browser or device changes.

sections.heading: Map-first behaviour
sections.body: The Toolkit avoids replacing MissionChief workflows. It adds operational summaries, overlays and shortcuts around the existing map and mission interfaces.

sections.heading: Mission Age Watch
sections.body: Use Mission Age Watch to identify ageing personal missions, inspect their current state and zoom or open the mission directly. The list remains time ordered, including missions that are clearing.

sections.heading: Critical View and Mission Inspector
sections.body: Critical View concentrates urgent work. Mission Inspector provides deeper context and is built on demand so it does not add unnecessary startup cost.

sections.heading: Transport Watcher
sections.body: Transport Watcher identifies transport-required missions and places its indicators alongside other active map overlays.

sections.heading: Coverage tools
sections.body: Coverage Heat Map and coverage rings provide geographic context for resource planning. Keep high-cost overlays disabled when they are not needed.

sections.heading: Smart Bookmark Labels
sections.body: Bookmark labels are shortened automatically using UK-aware abbreviations and a fallback compressor. The full name remains available through desktop tooltips or a touch long-press preview. A manual short label can override the generated result.

sections.heading: Import and export
sections.body: Use the Toolkit settings export to move configuration between Desktop, Tablet and iOS Mobile Mode. Export files are timestamped for easier recovery.

sections.heading: Reset strategy
sections.body: When diagnosing a persistent configuration problem, export settings first, reset only the affected feature where possible, and test with the default Map Command theme before applying a full reset.

troubleshooting.problem: MissionChief feels slow immediately after page load
steps: Confirm the installed Toolkit version matches the current version shown on this site.
Allow the idle bootstrap to complete before opening several large panels.
Temporarily disable high-cost overlays such as coverage visualisation.
Compare with the Toolkit disabled and include window.__MCMS_STARTUP_METRICS__ in a performance report.

troubleshooting.problem: A button or panel is missing
steps: Confirm the correct Desktop, Tablet or iOS operating mode is selected.
Check whether the toolbar is collapsed.
Open a structured bug report with browser, Tampermonkey and Toolkit versions.

troubleshooting.problem: Map markers or selectors stopped working after a game update
steps: Reload the page and reproduce the issue with the default theme.
Record the exact feature and mission or map context.
Submit the structured bug form so the changed MissionChief selector can be audited.

troubleshooting.problem: Settings did not carry to another device
steps: Export settings from the original device.
Import the complete timestamped export on the new device.
Confirm the target operating mode after import.
Reload MissionChief once after the import.

knownIssues.title: No release-blocking issue is recorded for the current verified release
knownIssues.body: The release dashboard currently records validation, Greasy Fork synchronisation, private backup, Discord publication and asset health as verified.

knownIssues.title: MissionChief interface changes can affect selectors
knownIssues.body: The Toolkit now validates static selectors and interface IDs in CI, but a live MissionChief markup change can still require a userscript update.

knownIssues.title: Large overlays have a deliberate performance cost
knownIssues.body: Coverage and monitoring systems should be enabled when operationally useful rather than left active by default on low-power devices.

mediaRoadmap: Map Command, Cyberpunk, Fallout, Umbrella and Factorio interface captures
